title: Carduus longispinus
intro: Carduus longispinus, also known as the long-spined thistle, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing pain and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Carduus longispinus include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Carduus longispinus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, sore throats, and other respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Carduus longispinus is classified as a species of thistle in the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the larger plant group of flowering plants. The genus Carduus is a group of thistles native to Europe and Asia. Carduus longispinus is specifically a perennial thistle species. Its classification also includes its position within the order Asterales.
